stimulus:
The following text is adapted from a nineteenth-century novel. Jonas is a young man making his way through the back country of upstate New York during the late eighteenth century.

He contracted himself out for six months, at the end of which he was to receive in payment a hundred and forty acres of cleared land lying near the Black River. [...] His employer proving false to the bargain in the matter of the land, and there being no court within fifty miles that would compel him to honor it, Jonas—too easily contented, perhaps, with whatever cheer the road and a kind stranger could supply—at length resigned the dispute and turned his face once more toward the south.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It implies that Jonas treasures a personality trait that is usually regarded as a flaw.
It suggests that if not for a certain aspect of his character, Jonas might not have been so easily thwarted in his ambition to acquire land.
It shows why Jonas could not have managed the labor of running a farm even if he had owned the necessary land.
It explains why Jonas is able to undertake courageous acts that others would generally avoid.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The underlined aside—"too easily contented, perhaps, with whatever cheer the road and a kind stranger could supply"—characterizes Jonas as a person whose easy contentment leaves him unwilling to press a legal dispute. Combined with his decision to resign the dispute and turn south, the underlined material attributes the loss of his land in part to this trait.

Choice A is incorrect because the narrator describes the trait critically, not as something Jonas treasures. Choice C is incorrect because no inability to do the labor of farming is suggested. Choice D is incorrect because the underlined portion accounts for Jonas's withdrawal from a fight, not for any courageous undertaking.
